chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.object: raw materials, components, or finished goods

triplets.subject: Suppliers

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is an atomic raw material or purchased part used in manufacturing that cannot be further broken down into simpler items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | Components are the building blocks used in manufacturing and are procured from external suppliers. | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Contact Email Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A contact email address is a specific data element used to store the email contact information of an individual or entity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | contact_email | VARCHAR(100) | | Contact email address |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspection Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The specific date on which an inspection is conducted, recorded for quality control and tracking purposes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Inspection date | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory represents the stock levels of components and products stored in warehouses, including records that track quantities and unique identifiers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date. | - Products are stored in Inventory records | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Timestamp
entities_post_resolution.definition: The last restock timestamp is the date and time when the inventory was most recently replenished.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp |

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing a product that acts as a parent in hierarchical or assembly structures within a product management system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | | - A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique product identifier | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |

entities_post_resolution.name: Planned Start Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: These entities represent the scheduled dates marking the start and end of a planned activity or process.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date | | | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product is any manufactured item, including finished goods, assemblies, and components, used in production and sales within the business system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Batches produce a specific Product | Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts. | - Products are used in Work Orders for production | - Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | - Products are stored in Inventory records | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Completed
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity of items or tasks that have been completed in a business process or transaction.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity To Produce
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity to produce refers to the planned or scheduled amount of product to be manufactured, whereas quantity produced indicates the actual amount of product manufactured.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_ordered | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity to produce | | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is the physical transfer of goods or materials between entities such as warehouses, suppliers, and customers within a supply chain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | - Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Technical Specifications
entities_post_resolution.definition: Technical specifications are detailed documents that define the technical requirements, standards, and criteria for materials, products, or components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | Specifications define requirements for Components | Specifications are used in Quality Control inspections | Specifications have version control for engineering changes | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

entities_post_resolution.name: Storage Capacity
entities_post_resolution.definition: Storage capacity refers to the maximum amount of data that can be stored in a storage device or system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK > 0 | Storage capacity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Street Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A street address is a specific location identifier used to pinpoint a physical location for mailing or navigation purposes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | Suppliers are evaluated based on performance ratings, preferred status for strategic partnerships, and maintain contact information for procurement operations. | Suppliers are associated with components they can provide through approved supplier relationships.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing an external vendor or provider that supplies goods or services to the organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | Suppliers are evaluated based on performance ratings, preferred status for strategic partnerships, and maintain contact information for procurement operations. | Suppliers are associated with components they can provide through approved supplier relationships. | | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name | | Stores external vendor information for component procurement. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Inventory Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier assigned to an inventory item, supplier, or warehouse to distinguish it within its respective domain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier | | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A warehouse is a physical location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored, managed, and organized within a supply chain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | | warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name | | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | Batches are stored in Warehouses | Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location |

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A work order is a formal document or record that authorizes and details the production or manufacturing of a specific quantity of a product within a manufacturing process.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Products are used in Work Orders for production | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Routes are used for scheduling Work Orders | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufactured item is any product or component that has been produced through a manufacturing process, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, and individual parts.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: A category of basic raw materials and purchased components that are procured externally and used in manufacturing processes.
synonyms: atomic raw materials or purchased parts
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Supplier Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: Component-related identifiers and associations used to manage and reference components, their products, and suppliers within a business system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) | | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | Junction table linking components to their approved suppliers with pricing and lead time information.

entities_post_resolution.name: Expiry Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: An expiry date is the date after which a product, especially perishable goods, should not be used or sold due to safety or quality concerns.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Product Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: Hierarchical product relationships are structured associations that define parent-child or multi-level connections among products or work orders within a manufacturing or product management system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Priority
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating the level of urgency or importance, typically with values like LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, and URGENT.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | priority | priority_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'MEDIUM' | LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: qc_type is a category representing different stages or types of quality control processes such as INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, and FINAL.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity On Hand
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity on hand is the current available amount of inventory items in stock, used for inventory management and order fulfillment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute that indicates the current state or phase of an entity or process within a system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED |

entities_post_resolution.name: Negotiated Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: Negotiated unit price is the agreed-upon price per unit of a product or service in a contract or transaction.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_price | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Negotiated unit price | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.)

entities_post_resolution.name: Defect Count
entities_post_resolution.definition: The defect_count represents the total number of defects identified during a quality inspection process.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Performance Rating
entities_post_resolution.definition: A performance rating is a numerical score, typically between 1.0 and 5.0, used to evaluate an individual's or entity's performance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | rating | DECIMAL(3,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 3.00, CHECK 1.0-5.0 | Performance rating |

entities_post_resolution.name: Procurement Lead Time
entities_post_resolution.definition: Procurement lead time is the period between initiating a purchase and receiving the goods or services.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time

entities_post_resolution.name: Reorder Trigger Level
entities_post_resolution.definition: Reorder trigger level is the inventory threshold at which a reorder is automatically initiated to replenish stock.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level |

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufacturing Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufacturing route is a sequence of operations or steps required to produce a product, including individual route steps and the overall set of routes used for production planning.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Route steps are ordered by sequence_number | Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation. | Routes determine production capacity requirements | Routes are used for scheduling Work Orders

entities_post_resolution.name: Cycle Time
entities_post_resolution.definition: Cycle time is the total time required to complete a process or operation from start to finish, including setup and processing durations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.

entities_post_resolution.name: Default 0.00
entities_post_resolution.definition: N/A
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: - A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | - A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies)
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | - A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies)

entities_post_resolution.name: Available Quantity
entities_post_resolution.definition: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Bom
entities_post_resolution.definition: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM entries support recursive relationships to represent multi-level product assemblies.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM entries support recursive relationships to represent multi-level product assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Bill Of Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: City Location
entities_post_resolution.definition: | city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Reference
entities_post_resolution.definition: | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |

entities_post_resolution.definition: | bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy |

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements.

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspector Reference
entities_post_resolution.definition: | inspector_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Inspector reference |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | inspector_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Inspector reference |

entities_post_resolution.definition: | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition |

entities_post_resolution.definition: | is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier flag |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier flag |

entities_post_resolution.name: Preferred Supplier For
entities_post_resolution.definition: | is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier for this component |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier for this component |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Reference
entities_post_resolution.definition: | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) |

entities_post_resolution.definition: | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |

entities_post_resolution.definition: | batch_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Production batch reference |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | batch_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Production batch reference |

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about products, including their identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships, costs, lead times, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about various components used in manufacturing or assembly processes, including their identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, costs, and related specifications... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: This table stores the components and structure of products as part of the Bill of Materials, detailing how individual parts assemble into finished products.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about suppliers providing goods or services to the company, including contact details and preference status.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about warehouse locations, their capacities, and management details.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: This table tracks inventory levels, component and product associations, and restocking information for warehouse management.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or maintenance work orders, including their status, priorities, associated products, quantities, and scheduled dates.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: This table records details about shipments, including their types, associated warehouses, suppliers, customers, and key dates related to shipment progress and status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: This table stores records of quality control inspections, including details about batch testing, inspection results, and defect counts to ensure product quality standards.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: This table stores specifications details, including versioning, effective dates, and critical parameters for product or process standards.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: This table records information about production batches of products, including quantities, production dates, warehouse locations, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or process routes, including the sequence of operations, associated work centers, and timing details for each route.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about the suppliers for each component, including whether they are preferred suppliers, the lead time for delivery, and the unit price of components.
